questions.text: Which directory in a role holds Jinja2 template files?
questions.explanation: The templates/ directory holds Jinja2 template files (.j2). These are processed by ansible.builtin.template and variables are substituted at runtime. The files/ directory holds static files used by ansible.builtin.copy.

questions.text: What is the difference between role defaults/ and vars/?

options.text: defaults/ are loaded first; vars/ are for templates only
defaults/ have the lowest precedence and are easily overridden; vars/ have higher precedence
vars/ are for global variables; defaults/ are for host variables

questions.explanation: defaults/main.yml has the lowest precedence of any variable source, making it the right place for values users are expected to override. vars/main.yml has higher precedence and is for role-internal constants. Always put user-configurable options in defaults/.

questions.text: Which command scaffolds a new role directory structure?
questions.explanation: ansible-galaxy role init <name> creates a complete role skeleton with all standard directories and placeholder files. This is the recommended way to start a new role as it ensures you follow Ansible's conventions.

questions.text: Where do you define role dependencies?
questions.explanation: Role dependencies are defined in meta/main.yml under the 'dependencies' key. Ansible processes dependencies before the role's own tasks. A requirements.yml file is used for installing roles from Galaxy, not for declaring dependencies between roles.

questions.text: When a role uses ansible.builtin.copy with src: myfile.txt, where does Ansible look for the file?
questions.explanation: Within a role, ansible.builtin.copy with a relative src path automatically looks in the role's files/ directory. Similarly, ansible.builtin.template looks in templates/. This is part of the role's self-contained structure that makes roles portable.

questions.text: How do you pass variables to a role when including it in a play?
questions.explanation: You can pass variables to a role using the dict syntax: { role: rolename, vars: { var: value } }. Alternatively, you can use include_role with vars. Role-level vars passed this way override the role's defaults but are lower priority than host/group_vars.

citations.label: Using roles at the play level

questions.text: What is the purpose of Ansible Galaxy?

options.text: A GUI for managing Ansible inventories
A public hub for sharing and downloading Ansible roles and collections
A cloud service that runs Ansible playbooks
A testing framework for Ansible roles

questions.explanation: Ansible Galaxy (galaxy.ansible.com) is the community hub for finding, downloading, and sharing Ansible roles and collections. You can install roles with 'ansible-galaxy role install geerlingguy.nginx' and collections with 'ansible-galaxy collection install community.general'.

questions.text: Which file in a role contains the main list of tasks to execute?
questions.explanation: Ansible automatically looks for tasks/main.yml as the entry point for a role's tasks. You can split tasks into multiple files and include them from tasks/main.yml using include_tasks or import_tasks for better organization.

questions.text: What does 'include_role' do differently from listing roles under 'roles:'?

options.text: include_role runs the role before any tasks; roles: runs it after
include_role is dynamic and can be used inside tasks with conditionals; roles: is static and runs before tasks

questions.explanation: include_role is a dynamic include that runs in-line within the task list and supports conditionals (when), loops, and variable passing at the task level. Listing under roles: is a static import that always runs before tasks: in the play and cannot be conditionally skipped per-host.

questions.text: Which file would you create to install multiple Galaxy roles at once?
questions.explanation: A requirements.yml file lists roles and collections to install with 'ansible-galaxy install -r requirements.yml'. It can include roles from Galaxy, GitHub, or local paths, and collections from Galaxy or private Automation Hub.
